sn3.7:1.2: As he was sitting to one side, King Pasenadi Kosala said to the Blessed One:
sn3.7:1.3: “Just now, lord, as I was sitting in judgment, I saw that even affluent nobles, affluent brahmans, & affluent householders—rich, with great wealth & property, with vast amounts of gold & silver, vast amounts of valuables & commodities, vast amounts of wealth & grain—tell deliberate lies with sensuality as the cause, sensuality as the reason, simply for the sake of sensuality.
sn3.7:1.4: Then, the thought occurred to me:
sn3.7:1.5: ‘I’ve had enough of this judging! Let some other fine fellow be known for his judgments!’”
sn3.7:2.1: “That’s the way it is, great king! That’s the way it is!
sn3.7:2.2: Even affluent nobles, affluent brahmans, & affluent householders… tell deliberate lies with sensuality as the cause, sensuality as the reason, simply for the sake of sensuality.
sn3.7:2.3: That will lead to their long-term harm & pain.”
sn3.7:2.4: That is what the Blessed One said. Having said that, the One Well-Gone, the Teacher, said further:
sn3.7:3.3: they don’t awaken to the fact that they’ve gone too far—
sn3.7:3.4: like fish into a trap set out.
